You're obsessed with finding the "winning product."

The guys printing $100K months are obsessed with finding winning problems.

Back pain doesn't take a season off. Insecurity doesn't follow trends. Bad sleep isn't going anywhere.

Find a problem that exists forever and you'll

The most dangerous phrase in entrepreneurship is "I already know that."

The moment you stop learning is the moment you start losing.

I've seen guys doing $300K/month still taking courses, still in masterminds, still asking questions like beginners.

The most dangerous part of this game is the first $10K.

Because it's enough to make you think you know what you're doing...

but not enough to survive the mistakes that overconfidence creates.

The winners stay paranoid. The ones who relax get humbled fast

Every beginner thinks they need a unique product nobody's ever seen before.

Then they see a kid doing $40K/month selling cable organizers and realize maybe they're overthinking this whole thing.
